sections.paragraph:
fut. יָצוּר, apoc. וַיָּצַר (like the kindred root צָרַר), to straiten, to press upon, to compress; hence-

(1) to bind together (into a bundle or roll), i.q. צָרַר No. 1. Pret. צַרְתָּ Deu 14:25 וַיָּצֻרוּ 2Ki 12:11 וַיָּצַר 5:23 .

(2) to press with siege, to besiege a city, followed by an acc. of the city, 1Ch 20:1 more often followed by עַל Deu 20:12 Eze 4:3 Dan 1:1 אֶל Deu 20:19 absol. Isai. 21:2 also followed by עַל 2Sa 20:15 and אֶל 1Sa 23:8, of the person besieged in the city. Once with acc. and עַל of the city, Isa 29:3, צַרְתִּי עָלַיִךְ מֻצָּב i.e. “I will push forward hosts (of soldiers) against thee” (ich fchiebe vor gegen dich). Metaph. Psa 139:5, אָחֹור וָקֶדֶם צַרְתָּנִי “thou besettest me on every side,” so that I cannot flee from thee.

(3) to urge, to press upon any one in pursuit, i.q. צָרַר No. 4, Deu 2:9 Exo 23:22. Part. צָרִים Est 8:11 also to stir up any one, to urge him to rise, Jud. 9:31 .

(4) to cut (pr. to press with a knife, compare גָּדַד to press, to cut), to divide. Arab. صار Med. Waw id. Hence צוּר edge.

(5) to form, i.q. יָצַר (derived from the idea of cutting), Arab. صَوَّرَ id., fut. וַיָּצַר Exo 32:4 1Ki 7:15 and אֲצוּרְךָ Jer 1:5 בתיב. (Aram. id.)

Derivatives, צוּרִישַׁדַּי־צוּר, צָר, צֵר, צִיר, צֹר, מָצֹור, מְצוּרָה, צַוָּאר.

צור

m.-

(1) stone, pebble (so called as being a compact solid mass. Others take it as pr. a piece of rock, see No. 2), Isa 8:14. Job 22:24, צוּר נְחָלִים “the pebbles of the brooks” (compare Gramm. § 106, 3, c).

(2) a rock, Job 18:4 24:8 metaph. used of God, the refuge and protection of Israel, Isa 30:29 Deu 32:37, “the rock where they took refuge;” Psa 18:3, 32 Psa 18:32, 47 Psa 18:47. By another figure (drawn from a quarry), it is applied to the founder of a nation. Isa 51:1. Pl. צוּרֹות Job 28:10.

(3) edge, from the root No. 4. Psa 89:44, צוּר חֶרֶב “the edge of a sword,” according to which analogy, Jos 5:2, Jos 5:3, חַרְבֹות צוּרִים are sharp knives, compare צֹר Exo 4:25 and so Ch. But LXX., Vulg., Syr., Arab. understand it to mean knives made of stone (compare צוּר No. 1) which the ancient Orientals used in castrating and circumcising. I wonder that those who hold this opinion (such as Maurer on Jos. loc. cit.) should have neglected the words added by the LXX. Jos 24:30, ἐκεῖ ἔθηκαν εἰς τὸ μνῆμα εἰς ὃ ἔθαψαν αὐτὸν (Joshua) ἐκεῖ τὰς μαχαίρας τὰς πετρίνας, ἐν αἷς περιέτεμε τοὺς υἱοὺς Ἰσραήλ … καὶ ἐκεῖ εἰσιν ἕως τῆς σήμερον ἡμέρας. This is worthy of remark, inasmuch as it clearly proves that stone knives were found in Palestine, as well as in Germany. Hence-

(4) form, Psa 49:15 קרי; French taille, from tailler; from the root No. 4, 5.

(5) [Zur], pr.n. m.-(a) of a leader of the Midianites, Num 25:15 31:8 Jos 13:21.-(b) 1Ch 8:30 9:36 .
